extraction.one_line_summary: VAN AERT Wout won the sprint after being completely isolated and forced to chase solo in the final 8km when breakaway partners were caught
tactical_patterns: Van Aert bridged solo to Tratnik/Piccolo move while entire chase group failed to organize, revealing asymmetric strength advantage
Lotto-Soudal executed full leadout for De Lie chasing UCI relegation points, creating sprint setup despite no team interest
Cosnefroy led out Naesen from third wheel position at 1km, AG2R defending title with unconventional positioning
Tratnik attacked when group swelled to 15 riders approaching finishing circuit, correctly identifying cooperation breakdown

rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: won despite complete isolation and visibly tiring from solo chase work in final 8km
rider_signals.LAURANCE Axel: positive: 21-year-old B&B Hotels rider took second ahead of established classics riders, emerging threat
rider_signals.DE LIE Arnaud: neutral: had full leadout but finished fourth, suggests weakness converting team support to wins
rider_signals.TRATNIK Jan: positive: correctly identified 15-rider group dysfunction and attacked solo with climbing on approach to circuit
rider_signals.GIRMAY Biniam: negative: only sixth despite being positioned well in final sprint with strong riders leadout
